title: Synthesis causing branch instructions to misbehave on RISC-V CPU

features.name: partially_dontcare_values_in_combinational_fsm
features.description: Code should include a combinational always block with default assignments using partially-defined values (x/dont-care) such as 2'b1x or 3'bxxx before a case statement that overrides them for specific states
features.snippet:
always @* begin
  cede=0; instr_we=0; rf_we=0; pc_we=0;
  alu_op=2'bxx; a_sel=2'bxx; b_sel=2'bxx; wd_sel=3'bxxx; pc_sel=2'bxx;
  case (cpu_state)
  S_BRANCH: begin
    if (branch) begin pc_sel=2'b1x; pc_we=1; end
  end
  endcase
end

features.name: branch_control_dependent_on_instr_bit
features.description: Code should include a branch control unit where pc_we (program counter write enable) is conditionally set based on a branch signal, with pc_sel assigned a partially-defined value 2'b1x that could cause synthesis to incorrectly infer dependency on instruction bits

features.name: cpu_control_signals_default_undefined
features.description: Code should include a CPU control unit where multiple control signals (alu_op, a_sel, b_sel, wd_sel, pc_sel) are initialized to dont-care values (x) in a default combinational block before being overridden in specific FSM states
